title: PluginsRegistry
description: Two shapes in one file: (1) the per-plugin manifest that authors ship as `plugin.json` (see `$defs/PluginManifest`); (2) the aggregate registry the implementation produces on disk (e.g. `~/.skill-map/plugins.json`), which lists all discovered plugins with their compat status. Both shapes are normative. camelCase keys throughout.

specCompat.description: Semver range this plugin is compatible with (e.g. `^1.0.0`, `>=0.3.0 <0.4.0`). Checked via `semver.satisfies(specVersion, this)` at load time.

extensions.description: Relative paths to extension files, resolved from the plugin directory.

granularity.description: Toggle granularity for this plugin. `bundle` (default) — the plugin id is the only enable/disable key; the whole set of extensions follows the toggle. `extension` — each extension is independently toggle-able under its qualified id `<plugin-id>/<extension-id>`. Built-in bundles use the same field: the `claude` bundle is `bundle` (the adapter and its kind-aware detectors form a coherent provider); the `core` bundle is `extension` (every kernel built-in is removable per the spec promise that no extension is privileged). Plugin authors should keep the default unless their plugin ships several orthogonal capabilities a user might reasonably want piecemeal.

storage.description: Persistence mode for this plugin. Absent = plugin does not persist state.

mode.description: Shared `state_plugin_kvs` table, scoped by plugin id.

path.description: Absolute path to the plugin directory.

status.description: Resolved state after discovery. `disabled` = user-disabled via config; `id-collision` = two plugins (any combination of project / global / --plugin-dir) declared the same `id`, both blocked, no precedence; others = automatic.

statusReason.description: Human-readable detail when status is anything other than `enabled`.
